states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection state before any command

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated; waiting for a transfer command

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for RNTO

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Authentication (USER/PASS cycle resulting in 230 response) must occur before LIST, RETR, STOR, or NLST.
A data connection must be established via PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT before a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST) is issued.
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
The RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
Upon authentication failure (530), the client may restart the sequence with the USER command.
PORT.host-port-params: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components)

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Confirmed by LLM spec analysis of standard FTP behavior where USER 331 triggers PASS requirement.

status_changes.description: Authentication (USER/PASS cycle resulting in 230 response) must occur before LIST, RETR, STOR, or NLST.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP access control verified by spec agent documentation review.

status_changes.description: A data connection must be established via PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT before a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST) is issued.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP protocol requirement for data channel setup confirmed by documentation analysis.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
status_changes.rationale: Documented as a global command in FTP specifications.

status_changes.description: The RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: Renaming sequence is a documented two-part transaction in FTP (RFC 959).

status_changes.description: Upon authentication failure (530), the client may restart the sequence with the USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Observed in session traces where failed PASS allows retrying with USER.
Confirmed by field analysis and protocol spec.
